title: fabric install --global ignores the declared --dry-run no-write contract
description: install-v2 declares --dry-run as printing the install plan without writing files, but runInitCommand checks args.global first and immediately calls runGlobalInstall({url}) without passing or honoring dry-run. runGlobalInstall then runs installGlobalCore, writes global ~/.fabric state, and mounts/clones a URL when provided. A user running fabric install --global --dry-run or --global --url ... --dry-run gets real global writes despite the shared dry-run flag contract.
fix_direction: Either reject --dry-run combined with --global as an explicit invalid combination, or extend runGlobalInstall with a planOnly/dryRun mode that reports global config/store clone/mount actions without writing. Add a CLI-level regression for fabric install --global --dry-run asserting no ~/.fabric writes and no git clone.

context.notes: Discovered by Round 3 reverse-oracle local dedup DBP-20260609-031000

title: fabric sync accepts --continue and --abort together and silently prioritizes continue
description: sync exposes two mutually exclusive resume actions, --continue and --abort, but the command handler only checks args.continue first and returns before inspecting args.abort. If both flags are supplied, the abort request is ignored and runContinueSync executes. This contradicts the documented conflict-resolution model where continue and abort are alternatives, and can run the more state-advancing path when the user also requested abort.
fix_direction: Add an explicit pre-dispatch guard: if args.continue && args.abort, print an actionable usage error and set a nonzero exit before calling either resume path. Cover the command-level flag combination in tests, not only the lower-level state-machine helpers.

context.notes: Discovered by Round 3 reverse-oracle local dedup DBP-20260609-031000

issue_history.note: Registered by Round 3 local dedup fallback after worker report failure

title: Quoted frontmatter arrays do not round-trip through line-based parsers
description: FabReview/FabExtract schemas accept arbitrary strings for tags and relevance_paths, and review also accepts title/summary. Writers now quote complex values with JSON/YAML escaping, but readers still use line-based helpers that split flow arrays on every comma and only strip surrounding quotes. A schema-valid value such as a tag or path containing a comma is serialized as a quoted array element, then parsed back as two corrupted elements; escaped newlines/backslashes in quoted title/summary similarly come back as literal escape text rather than the original string. This breaks list/search filters, path relevance, and metadata round-trip while being distinct from the older unescaped-injection findings.
fix_direction: Replace parseFlowArray/extractInlineArray and stripQuotes/unquote with a real YAML frontmatter parser or a tokenizer that respects quoted scalars and JSON/YAML escapes; add round-trip tests for commas, quotes, backslashes, and escaped newlines. Alternatively constrain schema values to the subset the current parser can actually round-trip.

context.notes: Discovered by Round 3 reverse-oracle local dedup DBP-20260609-031000

title: knowledge_deferred ledger events do not identify the deferred entry
description: fab_review.defer mutates each pending file, but the emitted knowledge_deferred event only contains timestamp plus optional until/reason. The schema has only optional stable_id and no pending_path, and the service never sets stable_id. When defer is called on one or more unassigned pending entries, the audit ledger cannot reconstruct which entry was deferred; with omitted reason/until the events are indistinguishable.
fix_direction: Add an explicit pending_path or knowledge_ref field to knowledge_deferred events, populate it for every deferred path, and include stable_id when frontmatter has one. Add regression coverage for multi-path defer and omitted until/reason.

context.notes: Discovered by Round 3 reverse-oracle local dedup DBP-20260609-031000

title: fab_review search cannot surface rejected entries despite include_rejected contract
description: The shared contract says rejected entries are excluded from list/search unless filters.include_rejected=true, and listPending adds the sibling rejected/ directory when that flag is set. searchEntries, however, builds sources only from pending and canonical roots, so rejected entries moved out of pending/ by reject are invisible to search even for audit tooling that opts into include_rejected.
fix_direction: Make searchEntries include the rejected sibling roots when include_rejected is true, and decide whether the output area enum needs a rejected value or a documented pending-with-status representation. Add tests covering reject -> search(include_rejected:true).

context.notes: Discovered by Round 3 reverse-oracle local dedup DBP-20260609-031000

issue_history.note: Registered by Round 3 local dedup fallback after worker report failure

title: Parity matrix E2E omits installed hooks and skills from the generated-output sweep
description: The install source of truth now writes fabric-audit and fabric-connect skills plus cite-policy, SessionEnd, and PostToolUse hook scripts/config commands across clients, but the parity matrix only lists three hook capabilities and skills through fabric-store, and the matrix-driven E2E maps only those same subsets. A drift in those installed generated outputs can therefore pass the advertised five-client parity gate because the canonical matrix never enumerates them.
fix_direction: Add parity-matrix capability rows for fabric-audit, fabric-connect, cite-policy-evict, session-end-marker, and post-tooluse-mutation, or explicitly mark them internal/non-matrix with a tested exclusion. Extend HOOK_SCRIPT/SKILL_SLUG and assertions so the E2E gate derives from the full install destination tables instead of a hand-maintained subset.

context.notes: Discovered by Round 3 reverse-oracle local dedup DBP-20260609-031000

issue_history.note: Registered by Round 3 local dedup fallback after worker report failure

title: Windows smoke bypasses the published CLI bin and promised --help coverage
description: The Windows smoke job says it catches POSIX-only path/shebang assumptions and covers CLI --help/--version, but the only CLI command executed is `node packages/cli/dist/index.js --version`. That bypasses the package `bin` entry and shebang path that npm/pnpm users actually invoke, and it never exercises help rendering on Windows, so package-bin or Windows-only help regressions can ship while the smoke still passes.
fix_direction: Run the installed package binary path on Windows, for example `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli exec fabric --version` plus `fabric --help`, or after build invoke the generated npm bin shim. Keep the comment and smoke command aligned so package metadata/bin and help rendering are actually covered.

context.notes: Discovered by Round 3 reverse-oracle local dedup DBP-20260609-031000
